name: Rhubarb Mousse
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Raspberry liqueur (or other fruit flavor)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, cook the sugar and water until it forms a thick syrup (the temperature should be 238°F).
2. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
3. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt and cream of tartar until they form soft peaks.
4. Gradually add the hot sugar syrup to the beaten egg whites, continuing to beat until well combined.
5. In another bowl, whip heavy cream until almost stiff. Stir in the raspberry liqueur.
6. Gently fold the whipped cream into the egg white-sugar mixture.
7. Transfer the mousse to a serving dish and refrigerate for 2 hours until set.
8. Remove from the refrigerator and stir in the pureed rhubarb.
9. If not serving immediately, keep the mousse chilled.
10. Serve in individual glasses or sherbet glasses, garnished with berries. Serves 8.
